economic_notes: First Nation operates Pelican Lake Mall and Store, Pelican Lake Electrical and Mechanical, Junior Farms Ltd., Pelican Lake Business Ventures, Pelican Lake Wild Rice Ltd (inactive), Spruce Creek Bison Ranch (inactive), Chitek Lake Indian Development Company (inactive), Pelican Lake Energy Corp (inactive), Pelican Lake Outfitters Ltd (inactive). COVID-19 impacted operations including reduced gaming revenues from First Nations Trust and reduced office hours.
notes: Financial statements are for fiscal year ended March 31, 2020 (not 2024). Auditor's report signed November 10, 2021 by MNP LLP in Prince Albert, Saskatchewan. The 2019 comparative year was audited by a different firm. COVID-19 impact noted. First Nation consolidates 11 entities including several inactive subsidiaries. The Nation is a member of Agency Chiefs Tribal Council (ACTC) and AC Child and Family Services Inc., which are not consolidated. Investments in Horizon Interiors Limited Partnership (39.95%), A.C. Forestry Ltd. (33%), and A.C. Realty Ltd. (33%) are carried at cost and are impaired. Contingency noted: 33.33% ownership in A.C. Forestry Ltd. with total available debt of $1,475,000; First Nation provided guarantee. Railway settlement claim loan and treaty annuities settlement claim loan outstanding. Encoded/garbled text throughout document made some figures difficult to extract precisely. The revenue figures shown include the combined total segment revenues which net to $19,698,455 for current year and $22,512,287 for prior year.
